caption: Ultrastructures of tubulointerstitial capillaries in the TIL model by transmission electron microscopy. Stratification of a thickened endothelial cytoplasm with vacuolation (arrows) and wide subendothelial space (asterisks) (panel a). Endothelium (arrows) detaching from a tubulointerstitial capillary with accumulation of collagen fibers around the capillary (asterisk) (panel b). Control kidneys showed normal tubulointerstitial capillaries and podocytes (panels c and d). All bars = 2 μm. Cap: capillary. End: endothelial cell. Glo: glomerulus. Pod: podocyt
answers: A1: The imaging modality represented in this image is Transmission Electron Microscopy.
A2: The image depicts the tubulointerstitial capillaries in the kidneys.

A3: Yes, abnormalities are identified in this image. These include stratification of a thickened endothelial cytoplasm with vacuolation and a wide subendothelial space. In addition, there is detachment of the endothelium from a tubulointerstitial capillary and accumulation of collagen fibers around the capillary.
A4: The image shows irregularities. The abnormalities stated above indicate that this image is not normal.

A5: Yes, this image contains labels that are significant. These include notations for capillary (Cap), endothelial cell (End), glomerulus (Glo), and podocyte (Pod). Arrows indicate vacuolation and detachment of the endothelium, while asterisks indicate wide subendothelial space and collagen fiber accumulation around the capillary. All the bars in the image are equal to 2 μm, which provides a scale for measurement.

caption: Example of original (top row) and enhanced images (bottom row). Subjective image quality increases from left to right. Original images were rated low quality mostly due to noise, absence of features, shadowing or blurriness. Highly rated images tend to show better contrast and lower noise levels, and often deeper penetration making the choroid more visible. In contrast, enhanced images show low to absent levels of noise in all images. In low quality cases, were the original image has an unusually low SNR, enhanced images tend to add blurriness or small artefacts. The contrast is higher than in original images, especially in the choroid.
answers: A1: The caption does not specify the imaging modality used for the images.
A2: The caption indicates the images depict the choroid, which is part of the eye.
A3: No specific abnormalities are mentioned in the image caption.

A4: The caption describes issues with the original images such as noise, absence of features, shadowing, or blurriness. Enhanced images can introduce blurriness or small artefacts in cases of low original signal-to-noise ratio (SNR).
A5: The caption does not mention any specific labels or indexes in the image, but it describes a subjective rating system for image quality.

caption: Fluorescence analysis of A. baumannii ATCC 19606 Δ3 single mutant with a Peredox plasmid. caption: Transgenic expression of acid ceramidase in CF mice abrogates ectopic surface expression of β1-integrin in CF mice. Confocal microscopy studies reveal that β1-integrin is absent from the luminal membrane of the bronchial epithelial cell surface in wild-type or Asah1tg mice, while CF mice accumulate β1-integrin in the luminal membrane of bronchial epithelial cells. The transgenic expression of acid ceramidase in CF-Asah1tg mice normalizes the expression of β1-integrin and abrogates its ectopic expression. The confocal microscopy studies were performed with Cy3-coupled anti-β1-integrin antibodies. Displayed are representative results from 6 independent studies each. Surface β1-integrin is indicated by arrows. Fluorescence was also quantified. The means ± SD are shown (n = 6). ***, P < 0.001 (by ANOVA and post hoc Student’s t test).

caption: Live confocal imaging experiments in CD1 mouse adrenal chromaffin cells expressing Doc2B WT, Doc2B ΔMID, Doc2B 6A or Doc2B DN fused to EGFP. Cells depolarization with High K+ solution (59 mM) triggers the recruitment of Doc2B WT, Doc2b ΔMID and in a lesser extent Doc2B DN to the plasma membrane. Mutations of the calcium binding residues of both C2 domains of Doc2B (Doc2B 6A) led to a permanent localization at the plasma membrane. Washing with extracellular solution (Wash) permits Doc2B WT and ΔMID to re-localize in the cytosol. Scale bar = 5 µ
answers: A1: The imaging modality represented in this image is live confocal microscopy.
A2: This image depicts adrenal chromaffin cells from a CD1 mouse.
A3: The caption does not mention any abnormalities identified in the image.

A4: The image shows irregularities in the case of Doc2B 6A mutation which leads to a permanent localization at the plasma membrane unlike other types of Doc2B that can re-localize in the cytosol after washing.
A5: Yes, the image contains a scale bar label that is significant. It measures 5 µm, and can be used to understand the actual size of the components in the image.
